name: Jacksonville North / St. Marys KOA Holiday
summary_text: A peaceful KOA Holiday just north of Jacksonville, Florida, offering spacious full-hookup sites, a small fishing lake, and an excellent overnight stop with convenient access to Cumberland Island, St. Marys, and Jacksonville.

siteFeatures.level: Mostly level pull-through and back-in sites.
otherNotes: Large pull-through sites are ideal for overnight travelers.
Quiet atmosphere despite its convenient location.

starlink.notes: Most sites have a fairly open southern sky with only moderate tree cover.

petNotes.walkingAreas: Wide campground roads and open grassy areas provide easy dog walking.
petNotes.dogFriendlyTrails: Nearby Crooked River State Park offers additional pet-friendly trails.
petNotes.petRestrictions: Pets must remain leashed outside the dog park.
petNotes.hazards: Mosquitoes can be heavy during warm weather. Be alert for snakes in natural areas.
petNotes.notes: A comfortable overnight stop with plenty of room to exercise dogs before getting back on the road.

hospital.name: Southeast Georgia Health System - Camden Campus
hospital.address: 2000 Dan Proctor Drive, St. Marys, GA 31558

tripTips: A great overnight stop along Interstate 95.
If time allows, spend a few hours exploring historic St. Marys.
Take the ferry to Cumberland Island if you're staying more than one night.
Fuel up before getting back on I-95.
Bring bug spray during warmer months.
